title: Trigger Editor for Menus
text: Trigger Editor for Menus The Trigger editor for Menus enables you to edit the ProcScript for drop-down menus and menu items. It features syntax highlighting, syntax checking, keyword completion, and context-sensitive help for ProcScript keywords. Menu items can have an option trigger and preDisplay trigger. Drop-down menus can have a preDisplay trigger. For more information, see trigger option and trigger preDisplay The Trigger Editor consists of the following resizable areas: Structure—view hierarchical structure of the current menu object. Script Editor—view and edit ProcScript in the current trigger. Tags—view and navigate to tagged triggers that match search criteria (when you use Find ) or contain errors (after a syntax check). Triggers—view and navigate to the Option and Predisplay triggers of the current menu option. The icon beside each trigger indicates whether the trigger contains content or not. Table 1. Trigger Icons Icon Meaning There are no triggers for the selected menu object. The trigger is empty. The trigger contains code. How to It is only possible to define triggers for menu options. To complete a keyword: Start typing a ProcScript keyword (such as a statement, function, precompiler directive, or compiler-time constant) Press Tab until the keyword you want to use is inserted. For example, when you type sel followed by Tab , the keyword selectcase is displayed. Press Tab again and selectdb is displayed. After the complete keyword list is displayed, the list is exited and a standard tab action is performed. To move backward in the list, press Shift + Tab . To get help about a ProcScript keyword, position the cursor on a ProcScript keyword and press F1 . The appropriate reference description is displayed in the Uniface Library. Toolbar Search: Column Icon Description Filter results... 22 rows found Table 2. ProcScript Editor Toolbar Icon Description Filter 22 rows found Filter 22 rows found Icon Description Open another menu. Save the current component. Make a copy of the current component. Add manual tag for current trigger. Check syntax of modified triggers. Check syntax of all triggers. Ctrl + Z Undo the most recent ProcScript edit, up to a maximum of 16 actions. It is not possible to undo typing that is immediately followed by a carriage return. Ctrl + Y Redo the most recently undone edit. You cannot undo an action that has been redone. Replace spaces by tabs in selected text. Replace tabs by spaces in the selected text. Add tab of each line in selected text. Remove first tab of each line in selected text. Convert the selected text to uppercase. Convert the selected text to lowercase. Format the ProcScript, adding indentation for readability. Convert the selected ProcScript to comments. Convert the selected comments to ProcScript code. Search the current ProcScript for a string. Find the next occurrence of the search string. Find a search string and replace it with another string. Find ProcScript in the component. Open the in a separate window for editing. Related Links ProcScript Syntax ProcScript provides high-level statements, functions, expressions, and operators that enable you to efficiently manipulate data and script application behavior. It is a powerful language that condenses complex processing into single commands. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
